name: Bat Cave and Bailon River Canyon - circular from Zuheros
name_original: Cueva de los Murciélagos y Cañón del Río Bailón – circular desde Zuheros
region_intro: Jogging around Zuheros offers routes through the Sierra Subbética Natural Park, characterized by limestone cliffs, extensive olive groves, and the Bailón River canyon. The region features varied terrain, from the relatively flat Vía Verde del Aceite to more challenging paths with significant elevation changes. These routes provide opportunities to experience the natural landscapes and historical landmarks of Andalusia. Best jogging routes around Zuheros The most popular jogging route is Bat Cave and Bailon River Canyon - circular from Zuheros, an 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This route explores the dramatic canyon and the area around the Cueva de los Murciélagos. Another top favourite among local runners is Plantío Tunnel – Steam locomotive - Mikado loop from Doña Mencía, a difficult 17.4 miles (28.1 km) path. This route follows sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite, passing through olive groves and historic railway architecture. Local runners also love the Doña Mencía Station – Zuheros Castle loop from Doña Mencía, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through the countryside towards Zuheros Castle, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Jogging around Zuheros is defined by limestone cliffs, olive groves, and the Vía Verde del Aceite. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Zuheros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 5 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Zuheros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Zuheros, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 90 running routes around Zuheros. The network offers a good variety, with 4 easy routes, 45 moderate routes, and 41 difficult routes, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Zuheros?
A: Yes, for families looking for an easier run, the Vía Verde del Aceite offers a relatively flat and accessible path. Sections of this route, which follows an old railway line through olive groves, are ideal for a relaxed jog with children. While specific family-friendly routes are not highlighted in the top picks, the 4 easy routes available provide suitable options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Zuheros?
A: Jogging routes in Zuheros feature diverse terrain, ranging from the relatively flat and well-maintained Vía Verde del Aceite, which winds through extensive olive groves, to more challenging paths within the Sierras Subbéticas Natural Park. These more difficult trails often involve significant elevation changes, limestone cliffs, and sections through the dramatic Cañon del Río Bailón.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Zuheros?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000997890/bat-cave-and-bailon-river-canyon-circular-from-zuheros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bat Cave and Bailon River Canyon - circular from Zuheros&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.2 miles (13.2 km) long and explores the dramatic canyon and the area around the Cueva de los Murciélagos.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features I can see while jogging in Zuheros?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You can jog past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zuheros Castle&lt;/a&gt;, explore the area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bat Cave&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3153745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreras de la Fuenseca&lt;/a&gt; waterfall. The Vía Verde del Aceite also features historic railway architecture and panoramic views of the olive groves.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Zuheros?
A: The running routes in Zuheros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 5 reviews. Over 500 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural landscapes, the well-maintained Vía Verde del Aceite, and the challenging yet rewarding trails through the Sierra Subbéticas Natural Park.

Q: Are there any running routes that include water features like waterfalls or rivers?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate natural water features. For instance, trails through the Cañon del Río Bailón often follow the river, and you can find the refreshing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3153745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorreras de la Fuenseca&lt;/a&gt; waterfall along certain paths, adding a scenic element to your run.

Q: Can I access jogging routes in Zuheros using public transport?
A: While Zuheros itself is a small village, the Vía Verde del Aceite, a major feature for runners, connects several towns. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31849366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plantío Tunnel – Steam locomotive - Mikado loop from Doña Mencía&lt;/a&gt;, start from nearby towns like Doña Mencía, which may have better public transport links. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Zuheros or nearby starting points.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic viewpoints of Zuheros or the surrounding area?
A: Absolutely. Many jogging routes lead to elevated points that provide breathtaking panoramic views. You can find excellent vistas of the village, its dramatic castle, and the sprawling olive groves that characterize the Andalusian landscape. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bat Cave&lt;/a&gt; also offers superb viewpoints.

Q: What is a good route for a longer, more challenging run in the area?
A: For a longer and more challenging run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31849366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plantío Tunnel – Steam locomotive - Mikado loop from Doña Mencía&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 17.4-mile (28.1 km) path follows sections of the Vía Verde del Aceite, offering a substantial distance through varied scenery, including olive groves and historic railway structures.

Q: Are there any routes that connect Zuheros with nearby towns or attractions?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde del Aceite is a key route that connects Zuheros with other towns along its path. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doña Mencía Station – Zuheros Castle loop from Doña Mencía&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.9-mile (11.2 km) trail that links Doña Mencía with Zuheros Castle, allowing runners to explore the countryside between these points.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Zuheros?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for jogging in Zuheros. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, making for comfortable running conditions. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are generally mild, some trails might be affected by rain.
tips: Impressive limestone walls, with a multitude of vegetation sprouting through its numerous pores. They have been formed over time by the force of the Bailón River. From some angles you can see Zuheros and his castle looming between the walls.

It is a prehistoric cave, the largest in the province of Córdoba, declared a Natural Monument in 2001. Its name comes from the bats, of different species, that inhabit this cave. In his tourist visit, a descent of up to 65 m deep is made.

From this point you have a very good perspective of the architectural homogeneity of the town of Zuheros, with its whitewashed houses with clay tile roofs forming narrow and winding streets. Only the castle that stands out in the background breaks the landscape. This is one of the best points from which to observe Zuheros, even when it rains, due to the perspective offered by the hill of the Sierra Subbética towards the incipient Cordoba countryside.
Construction located in the highest rocky part of the town whose purpose was to protect against any external invasion, whose views are worth enjoying.

waypoints.name: View of Zuheros Through the Cliffs of the Bailón River Gorge
